steps.title: Confirm scene safety
steps.description: Confirm scene is safe before approaching. Note any hazards. Don appropriate PPE.

steps.title: Assess consciousness level
steps.description: Assess response to verbal and painful stimuli. Record AVPU score. Determine if patient responds to voice, pain, or is completely unresponsive.

steps.title: Assess airway and breathing
steps.description: Confirm patent airway and adequate breathing. Check for: regular breathing pattern, SpO2, signs of respiratory depression (slow/irregular breathing, cyanosis). Note any vomit or foreign body in airway.

steps.title: Place in recovery position
steps.description: If breathing adequately and no spinal injury suspected: place in lateral (recovery) position. Ensure airway remains patent. Monitor breathing continuously.
steps.guideline_ref: Kenya BLS/ACLS Field Protocol — Recovery Position

steps.title: C-spine protection (if trauma suspected)
steps.description: If mechanism suggests spinal injury: maintain manual in-line stabilisation, apply cervical collar, and use log-roll technique. Do NOT place in recovery position until spinal clearance.

steps.title: Record initial vitals
steps.description: Record: RR, SpO2, BP (if equipment available), HR, consciousness (AVPU), temperature, GCS (if trained). Note blood glucose if glucometer available.

steps.title: Check blood glucose
steps.description: If glucometer available: check blood glucose. Hypoglycaemia (<4 mmol/L or <70 mg/dL) is a common reversible cause of reduced consciousness. Treat with IV glucose if available.

steps.title: Reassess vitals (every 5 min)
steps.description: Repeat vitals every 5 minutes. Monitor for deterioration: decreasing consciousness, respiratory depression, cardiovascular instability. Document any changes.

steps.title: Determine disposition
steps.description: Record destination facility. Ensure receiving facility notified of: consciousness level, vital sign trend, blood glucose result, suspected cause of unresponsiveness, and any treatments given.
